definition: Cross-asset markets are pricing mutually inconsistent sovereign debt outcomes. Equities (21-22x forward P/E) and credit (IG ~90-110bp) price benign fiscal trajectory, while rates (term premium 50-100bp+, positive 30Y-SPX correlation) and gold ($2,800-3,000+, CB buying >1,000t/year) price fiscal stress. At least two asset classes are mispricing the regime.
evidence: Equities at 21-22x forward P/E and IG credit at ~90-110bp price benign trajectory
Term premium 50-100bp+, positive 30Y-SPX correlation price fiscal stress
Gold at $2,800-3,000+ with CB buying >1,000t/year prices fiscal stress
Directly observable in market prices, not model output
open_questions: Which asset classes are mispricing — equities/credit or rates/gold?
Does the Kalecki-Levy identity make equity pricing internally consistent even under fiscal stress?
